name: White Sauce for Macaroni
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy pot, combine the milk, carrot, celery, onion, peppercorns, clove, and bay leaf.
2. Heat until bubbles form around the edge of the pot.
3. Set aside for 5 minutes and strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
4. Over moderate heat, melt the butter in a saucepan.
5. Add the flour and stir until the flour and butter are blended.
6. Cook over moderate heat for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
7. Gradually add the strained milk to the saucepan in a slow, steady stream while continuously stirring with a whisk.
8. Reduce the heat to low and continue to stir as the mixture comes up to a simmer.
9. Season to taste with nutmeg, salt, and freshly ground pepper.
10. Simmer for 5 to 10 minutes, stirring occasionally until the sauce becomes smooth and thick.
11. Makes approximately 1 1/2 cups. This Sauce Goes With Crusty Macaroni...
